headline: AI-assisted coding pulled ahead this month while token economics surfaced as a fresh cluster of breakouts.
body: AI-assisted coding was the clear leader, improving to momentum 145 against a field of lagging coding techniques like MCP, LSP, subagents, and code navigation, with the ROI of those tools now being weighed differently by bootstrapped versus funded founders [6]. A new token economics cluster broke out at once: tokenomics and token usage analysis entered as new techniques, token efficiency, model compression, and quantization broke out as tools, and TechCrunch framed a possible "Tokenpocalypse" of rising token pricing tied to AI IPOs [0]. LLM and memory systems also broke out, with one engineer arguing LLMs are eroding software engineering careers [9]. Cache mechanics drew attention as a question on how tunable thinking efforts break Claude's prompt cache but not Codex's circulated [10].

cited_items.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
The ROI of AI coding looks different when you are a bootstrapped founder
LLMs are eroding my software engineering career and I don't know what to do
Ask HN: How are thinking efforts implemented?
